ethernet.nav_path: Google Home app > Wi-Fi > Settings > Advanced Networking > WAN
steps_ipoe: Connect an Ethernet cable from the nbn connection box to the port marked with the globe icon on the bottom of the Nest WiFi Pro
Plug the power cable into the Nest WiFi Pro and wait about 1 minute until the light pulses white
If setting up for the first time: open the Google Home app, tap '+' > 'Set up device' > 'New device', select your home, and scan the QR code on the bottom of the device
The Nest WiFi Pro will auto-detect your connection type — for Belong (IPoE/DHCP), it should connect automatically with no manual configuration
If the router was previously configured for PPPoE (e.g. from TPG/iiNet): open the Google Home app, go to Wi-Fi > Settings > Advanced Networking > WAN
To change WAN settings, the router must be offline — disconnect the Ethernet cable from the nbn box and wait until the light pulses yellow
Reconnect the Ethernet cable and wait 3-5 minutes for the router to come online

wan_config.vlan_notes: No VLAN configuration available or needed. Google Home app does not expose VLAN settings.

physical.wan_port_position: On the underside of the device — the port marked with a globe icon
physical.wan_port_notes: Both ports are on the underside of your modem, not the back.
physical.lan_ports: 1 x Gigabit Ethernet (LAN, marked with double-arrow icon)
physical.reset_button: On the bottom of the device — a small circle etched into the surface

factory_reset.method: Turn the device over and locate the small reset circle on the bottom. Press and hold for 12 seconds. The light will flash yellow, then turn solid yellow. When solid yellow, release. The reset takes up to 5 minutes — do not unplug during this time. When complete, the light flashes white then blue, indicating the device is ready for fresh setup via Google Home app.
factory_reset.notes: Factory reset erases all settings and removes the device from your Google Home. You will need to set it up again from scratch through the Google Home app. There are no default credentials to restore — the device is always configured through the app.

firmware.notes: Google Nest WiFi Pro firmware updates are fully automatic and mandatory. There is no way to manually check, download, or install firmware. Updates happen silently in the background. This is a positive for Belong — the device is always on the latest firmware.

setup.setup_notes: The Nest WiFi Pro is the simplest possible setup for Belong customers: plug the Ethernet cable from the nbn box into the globe-icon port, power on, and it auto-detects IPoE/DHCP. No admin panel, no menu navigation, no dropdown changes. The only scenario requiring manual WAN configuration is if the device was previously used with a PPPoE ISP — in that case, the Google Home app must be used to change the WAN type to DHCP, which requires temporarily disconnecting the Ethernet cable. Ports are on the bottom of the device (unusual placement), not the rear — this is worth highlighting in the UI to avoid confusion.

setup_sources.description: SpinTel — Configuring Google Nest WiFi for NBN (step-by-step with screenshots)

Google Nest Help — Factory reset procedure
Google Nest Help — WAN vs LAN port identification
Google Nest Community — Port identification (globe = WAN, arrows = LAN)
